name: Function Sum
statement:
Suppose you have an integer array $a_1, a_2, \dots, a_n$.
Let $\operatorname{lsl}(i)$ be the number of indices $j$ ($1 \le j  \lt  i$) such that $a_j  \lt  a_i$.
Analogically, let $\operatorname{grr}(i)$ be the number of indices $j$ ($i  \lt  j \le n$) such that $a_j  \gt  a_i$.
Let's name position $i$ good in the array $a$ if $\operatorname{lsl}(i)  \lt  \operatorname{grr}(i)$.
Finally, let's define a function $f$ on array $a$ $f(a)$ as the sum of all $a_i$ such that $i$ is good in $a$.
Given two integers $n$ and $k$, find the sum of $f(a)$ over all arrays $a$ of size $n$ such that $1 \leq a_i \leq k$ for all $1 \leq i \leq n$ modulo $998\,244\,353$.
input: The first and only line contains two integers $n$ and $k$ ($1 \leq n \leq 50$; $2 \leq k  \lt  998\,244\,353$).
output: Output a single integer — the sum of $f$ over all arrays $a$ of size $n$ modulo $998\,244\,353$.
